title: 2026 Connecticut Governor Election
description: Connecticut is holding an election for governor on November 3, 2026, with the primary scheduled for August 11, 2026. The race is notable because Gov. Ned Lamont is seeking re-election and faces primary challenges in both parties, while Ballotpedia and major race-ratings outlets currently rate the general election as safely or solidly Democratic. The contest is likely to center on affordability, taxes, housing, education, and the state’s overall direction, with Democrats defending an incumbent record and Republicans arguing for a change in leadership.

polling.pollster: University of New Hampshire Survey Center
University of New Hampshire Survey Center

candidates.name: Ned Lamont
candidates.summary: Ned Lamont is the incumbent governor of Connecticut and is seeking another term in 2026. His campaign emphasizes affordability, tax relief, housing, workforce development, and continued investment in public services, while his official biography highlights his prior local government and investment-advisory experience.

summary_sources.title: Learn About Connecticut Governor Ned Lamont - CT.gov

career_history.title: Governor
career_history.description: Elected governor in 2018 and took office in 2019; re-elected in 2022.

career_history.title: Chairman
career_history.description: Served for four years overseeing the state pension fund, according to his official biography.

career_history.title: Member
career_history.organization: Greenwich Board of Selectmen and Board of Estimate and Taxation
career_history.description: Served in local government in Greenwich before becoming governor.

career_history.title: Founder
career_history.organization: A weekly newspaper in a Connecticut town
career_history.description: Founded a weekly newspaper early in his public-service career, according to the state biography.

candidates.name: Joshua Elliott
candidates.summary: Joshua Elliott is a Democratic state representative from Hamden and the main primary challenger to Gov. Ned Lamont. Public reporting describes him as a long-shot challenger who is running to offer a different direction within the Democratic Party, though the race remains heavily tilted toward the incumbent.

summary_sources.title: Connecticut gubernatorial and lieutenant gubernatorial election, 2026 - Ballotpedia
Poll: Lamont's long-shot challenger is far behind, but slightly less so

career_history.title: State Representative
career_history.description: Serves as a member of the Connecticut House of Representatives from Hamden.

candidates.name: Ryan Fazio
candidates.summary: Ryan Fazio is a Republican state senator from Greenwich and one of the leading contenders for the GOP nomination. Coverage and polling suggest he has relatively strong favorability among Republican primary voters, and his campaign is positioned as a more traditional Republican alternative in a field that also includes former New Britain Mayor Erin Stewart.

summary_sources.title: Connecticut gubernatorial and lieutenant gubernatorial election, 2026 - Ballotpedia
Poll: Lamont's long-shot challenger is far behind, but slightly less so

career_history.title: State Senator
career_history.description: Serves in the Connecticut State Senate representing Greenwich-area constituents.

candidates.name: Erin Stewart
candidates.summary: Erin Stewart is the former mayor of New Britain and a Republican candidate for governor in 2026. Her campaign biography emphasizes her record as a long-serving mayor focused on fiscal management, school and park investment, downtown development, and local public-safety and social-service initiatives.

summary_sources.title: Bio - Erin Stewart – Republican For Governor 2026

career_history.title: Mayor
career_history.description: Served as the 40th mayor of New Britain and was first elected at age 26.

career_history.title: Member
career_history.description: Served on the state higher-education governing board, according to her campaign biography.

career_history.title: Chairwoman
career_history.description: Led the regional council of governments.

career_history.title: Member
career_history.organization: Connecticut Conference of Municipalities Board of Directors
career_history.description: Served on the board of the municipal advocacy organization.

candidates.name: Betsy McCaughey
candidates.summary: Betsy McCaughey is a Republican candidate in the 2026 Connecticut governor primary and a late entrant into the race. She is best known nationally as a former New York lieutenant governor and as a media commentator, and early polling shows low name recognition among Connecticut Republican primary voters.

summary_sources.title: Connecticut gubernatorial and lieutenant gubernatorial election, 2026 - Ballotpedia
Poll: Lamont's long-shot challenger is far behind, but slightly less so

career_history.title: Lieutenant Governor
career_history.description: Served as New York's lieutenant governor.

career_history.title: Commentator
career_history.description: Has worked as a political commentator and media personality.

candidates.name: Timothy Wilcox
candidates.summary: Timothy Wilcox is a Republican candidate in the 2026 Connecticut governor primary. Ballotpedia and reporting indicate he is running a minimal campaign and has filed paperwork saying he would not be raising or spending money, making him a low-profile candidate in the field.

summary_sources.title: Connecticut gubernatorial and lieutenant gubernatorial election, 2026 - Ballotpedia
Poll: Lamont's long-shot challenger is far behind, but slightly less so
